R0146.title: Consumer Loan Fraud (Genuine Retroactive Payment)
R0146.definition: Fraudulent behavior of obtaining consumer loans by forging repayment capability through genuine retroactive payment of social security, housing provident fund, etc.
R0146.description: Criminal groups help borrowers make genuine retroactive payments for social security, housing provident fund and other records to forge proof of stable income and repayment capability, thereby fraudulently obtaining consumer loans from financial institutions. This method is more difficult to identify than traditional fake materials because the retroactive payment records are genuine. In 2026, this method has become mainstream, bringing new challenges to financial institution risk control.

references.title: New Changes in Consumer Loan Fraud in 2026: Genuine Retroactive Payment Becomes Mainstream Method
